pages.notes: Page contains one glued-in photograph showing a man cleaning/washing the windscreen of a white camper van at a campsite with trees in the background and a washing line visible. Very faint pencil handwriting appears in the top-left margin area of the page, largely illegible; possibly reads '1st Camp[er?]' and further lines below but cannot be read with confidence.

pages.full_text:
__May.__
First we spent a day having a picnic at __Malvern__, visiting a quilting exhibition.
Then we went to Kate & Jamie's __farm__ for one night, very warm and humid.
The van worked well. The boys played on bikes + the water shoot.
We forgot a first aid kit.

When first Simon showed the van to me, Peter was sprayed by the tap.
pages.notes: Year '1989' written in top-right corner alongside handwritten page number '1'. A small hand-drawn sketch appears at the bottom of the page illustrating a person (stick figure) being sprayed by a tap — this is an illustrative doodle accompanying the anecdote, not a route sketch. 'Kate & Jamie's farm' is an unidentified location (not a named place). The underlined word 'farm' appears to be underlined in the same way as 'Malvern' — both are underlined in the original. A blue sticker or ink mark '2' appears below the year annotation in the top-right corner.
